lesson.title: Lesson 85: Never Too Old To Learn

rights.notes: Migrated from legacy published content; migration does not establish redistribution rights.

segments.text: First listen, and then answer the question.

translation.text: 先听录音，然后回答问题。

segments.text: I have just received a letter from my old school, informing me that my former headmaster, Mr.

translation.text: 我刚收到母校的一封信，信中告诉我，前校长斯图尔特·佩奇先生。

segments.text: Stuart Page, will be retiring next week.
Pupils of the school, old and new, will be sending him a present to mark the occasion.

translation.text: 学校的新老学生将送给他一件礼物，以纪念这一时刻。

segments.text: All those who have contributed towards the gift will sign their names in a large album which will be sent to the headmaster's home.

translation.text: 所有为礼物出资的人都会在一本大纪念册上签名，纪念册将被送到校长家中。

segments.text: We shall all remember Mr. Page

translation.text: 我们都会记得佩奇先生。

segments.text: for his patience and understanding, and for the kindly encouragement he gave us when we went so unwillingly to school.

translation.text: 记得他的耐心和善解人意，也记得我们当年极不情愿地去上学时，他给予我们的亲切鼓励。

segments.text: A great many former pupils will be attending a farewell dinner in his honor next Thursday.

translation.text: 许多往届学生将参加下周四为他举行的欢送晚宴。

segments.text: It is a curious coincidence that the day before his retirement, Mr. Page

translation.text: 一个奇妙的巧合是：退休前一天，佩奇先生。

segments.text: will have been teaching for a total of forty years.
After he has retired, he will devote himself to gardening.

translation.text: 退休后，他将专心从事园艺。

segments.text: For him, this will be an entirely new hobby.

translation.text: 对他而言，这将是一项全新的爱好。

segments.text: But this does not matter, for, as he has often remarked, one is never too old to learn.

translation.text: 不过这并不要紧，因为正如他常说的那样，人永远不会老到学不了新东西。
